result:
I'd make every human being incapable of lying to themselves. 

Self-deception is the root of most avoidable suffering—people convincing themselves their bad habits aren't harming them, their political beliefs are immune to evidence, their relationships are fine when they're not, or that short-term comfort matters more than long-term consequences. If that mechanism broke, everything else would shift faster than any policy, technology, or ideology could force.

message.content:

Self-deception is the root of most avoidable suffering—people convincing themselves their bad habits aren't harming them, their political beliefs are immune to evidence, their relationships are fine when they're not, or that short-term comfort matters more than long-term consequences. If that mechanism broke, everything else would shift faster than any policy, technology, or ideology could force.
message.reasoning_content: If I could wave a magic wand and alter the world in just one way, I'd make sure that every single person had an innate, unshakeable drive to seek and embrace the truth, no matter how tough or uncomfortable it might be.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
